title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: file gives AI coding agent context needed help user run recorderFeedback package end end.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: Use repository generate personalized feedback content recipients, optionally dispatch via SMTP email.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: R package workflow helpers templates. Typical user flow project initialization, data loading, rendering, dispatch.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: rf_init(path = "."): scaffold project folder. rf_get_recipients(): run configured script update recipients CSV. rf_get_data(): run configured script update records CSV. rf_verify_data(verbose = TRUE): verify required columns data readiness. rf_render_single(recipient_id = 1): render one recipient debugging. rf_render_all(batch_id): run targets pipeline full batch. rf_verify_batch(batch_id, verbose = TRUE): validate rendered outputs metadata. rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id): send rendered content email.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: package expects initialized projects contain: config.yml _targets.R run_pipeline.R scripts/get_recipients.R scripts/get_data.R scripts/focal_filter.R scripts/recipient_select.R scripts/computation.R templates/content.Rmd templates/template.html templates/email_format.R data/ CSV inputs renders/ generated outputs

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: config.yml (default profile): recipients_file, recipients_script data_file, data_script focal_filter_script recipient_select_script (optional selective rendering hook) computation_script_bg, computation_script_focal content_template_file, html_template_file, email_format test_mode, mail_* keys configured file missing, agent stop report missing path clear fix.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: rf_render_all(batch_id) supports selection via recipient_select_script. script must define: recipient_select(recipients, data, config) Accepted return values: NULL: render recipients. Logical vector (length nrow(recipients)). Vector recipient_id values include. Data frame recipient_id, selected, optional skip_reason. Expected behavior: Selected recipients rendered. Non-selected recipients appear renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv render_status = "skipped" HTML file. Failed renders appear render_status = "failed".

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: user asks generate feedback, follow order: Confirm repository root config.yml exists. Load package config. rf_get_recipients() rf_get_data() rf_verify_data(TRUE) rf_render_single(recipient_id = <known_id>) choose batch_id (timestamp-safe, unique) run rf_render_all(batch_id) rf_verify_batch(batch_id, TRUE) inspect renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv SMTP configured user confirms, run rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id)

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: successful rendering: Folder exists: renders/<batch_id>/ File exists: renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv Per-recipient HTML files exist render_status == "rendered" meta_table.csv contains recipient_id, file, status fields

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: meta_table.csv missing: rendering complete. Check targets::tar_meta(fields = "error", complete_only = TRUE). single recipient fails batch: reproduce rf_render_single(). emails skipped unexpectedly: inspect recipient_select_script render_status values meta table. recipient counts mismatch: compare config$recipients_file meta_table.csv check skipped recipients.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
text: Prefer test_mode: TRUE developing email steps. run SMTP dispatch without explicit user confirmation. Use unique batch_id values avoid confusion prior renders. delete existing render directories unless user asks.

title: RecorderFeedback Agent Helper
code: library(recorderFeedback)  rf_get_recipients() rf_get_data() rf_verify_data(TRUE)  batch_id <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S") rf_render_all(batch_id) rf_verify_batch(batch_id, TRUE)  meta <- read.csv(file.path("renders", batch_id, "meta_table.csv")) head(meta) # rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id)  # optional

title: Distributing Content
text: vignette explains safely send generated feedback email. designed new users already rendered batch successfully.

title: Distributing Content
text: Confirm prerequisites first: run rf_render_all(batch_id). renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv exists. rf_verify_batch(batch_id) completes without blocking errors. can run one preflight command rendering dispatching:
code: # Fail fast before render rf_preflight(stage = "render")  # Fail fast before dispatch rf_preflight(stage = "dispatch", batch_id = batch_id)

title: Distributing Content
text: Distribution settings managed project’s config.yml file. Key entries include: mail_server: SMTP server address. mail_port: SMTP port (default: 587). mail_use_tls / mail_use_ssl: Security options email. mail_username, mail_password: Credentials authentication. mail_sender, mail_name: Sender details. mail_subject: Subject line feedback emails. mail_subject_template: Subject template supporting variables like {{name}}, {{recipient_id}}, {{campaign_name}}, {{batch_id}}. mail_body_prefix_template, mail_body_suffix_template: Optional HTML blocks inserted /rendered content variable support. mail_test_recipient: Address test emails. mail_creds: Authentication mode ("anonymous" "envvar"). test_mode: TRUE, messages redirected mail_test_recipient. mail_retry_max: Number retries first failed send attempt. mail_retry_backoff_sec: Base backoff seconds (uses exponential backoff). mail_rate_per_minute: Maximum send attempts per minute. mail_attachments_col: Column name meta_table.csv containing attachment paths (delimited ,, ;, |). mail_inline_images_col: Column name meta_table.csv containing inline image paths (delimited ,, ;, |). campaign_name, campaign_metadata: Campaign context used templates.

title: Distributing Content
text: generating feedback content, use rf_dispatch_smtp() send emails recipients. function uses configuration connect mail server distribute feedback. preflight check without sending emails: continue previously interrupted run skip already successful sends: Preview sample without sending emails: Preview specific recipients: safe first run, keep test_mode: TRUE config.yml. sends outputs mail_test_recipient instead real recipient addresses. test_mode: FALSE, must explicitly confirm live send:
code: batch_id <- "example_batch" rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id) rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, dry_run = TRUE) rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, resume = TRUE) rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, preview_only = TRUE, preview_n = 5) rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, preview_only = TRUE, preview_recipient_ids = c(101, 205, 309)) rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, confirm_live_send = TRUE)

title: Distributing Content
text: Email body content comes content_template_file html_template_file. Sending format controlled email_format (usually templates/email_format.R). Subject/body templating tokens include recipient fields meta_table.csv plus campaign tokens {{campaign_name}}, {{campaign_date}}, {{dispatch_timestamp}}, {{batch_id}}. include attachments/inline images, add columns meta_table.csv matching mail_attachments_col mail_inline_images_col file paths.

title: Distributing Content
text: dispatch, can verify emails sent check errors: Inspect generated dispatch files: renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_log.csv: One row per recipient send attempt. renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_summary.csv: Summary metrics run. renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_preview/preview_index.csv: Preview index preview mode used.

title: Distributing Content
text: Authentication failure: review mail_creds, username/password, host, port. TLS/SSL mismatch: check mail_use_tls mail_use_ssl SMTP provider. Footer email mismatch error: generated file email match meta table row. QA failures rf_verify_data(): duplicate recipient_id, missing/invalid emails, optional recorder schema checks (date/species/site).

title: Distributing Content
text: Email distribution fully configurable via config.yml. Use rf_dispatch_smtp() send feedback recipients. Keep test_mode: TRUE dry runs, switch FALSE live sending. approach helps validate delivery safely contacting real recipients.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: end--end example shows use recorderFeedback iNaturalist data. written new users, stage explicit: initialise project, load recipients, fetch observations, render content, distribute.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: end : recipients table linked iNaturalist user IDs. records table fetched iNaturalist API. simple personalised report template. One test render one batch render. Optional SMTP dispatch.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: Initialise project directory read config: Run subsequent commands project directory relative paths config.yml valid.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: Recipients individuals feedback reports generated. example, manually define script. File: scripts/get_recipients.R Run recipient script: Inspect recipients:
code: config <- config::get() df <- data.frame(   email = c("simon@example.com"),   name = c("Simon"),   recipient_id = c("1152941") # iNaturalist user ID ) write.csv(df, config$recipients_file, row.names = FALSE) rf_get_recipients() read.csv(config$recipients_file)

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: now pull iNaturalist observations recipient last 30 days. Install required packages needed: File: scripts/get_data.R Run verify:
code: install.packages(c("httr", "jsonlite")) library(httr) library(jsonlite)  get_inat_observations <- function(user_id) {   today <- Sys.Date()   start_date <- today - 30      base_url <- "https://api.inaturalist.org/v1/observations"   query <- list(     user_id = user_id,     d1 = start_date,     d2 = today,     per_page = 30,     order = "desc",     order_by = "created_at"   )      response <- GET(base_url, query = query)   stop_for_status(response)      data <- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")   obs <- fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE)      output <- obs$results[, c(     "id",     "user.id",     "observed_on",     "taxon.preferred_common_name",     "taxon.name",     "taxon.iconic_taxon_name"   )]   names(output) <- c(     "record_id",     "recipient_id",     "date",     "common_name",     "scientific_name",     "species_group"   )      return(output) }  recipients <- read.csv(config$recipients_file) observations <- data.frame() for (i in seq_len(nrow(recipients))) {   observations <- rbind(observations,                         get_inat_observations(recipients$recipient_id[i])) }  write.csv(observations, config$data_file, row.names = FALSE) rf_get_data() rf_verify_data(verbose = TRUE)

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: build simple R Markdown template generates personalized summary recipient. template includes: Greeting recipient name Count unique species recorded table recent observations (keep vignette self-contained, template text printed directly.)
code: #> --- #>  title: "iNaturalist Summary" #>  params: #>    recipient_name: "" #>    recipient_email: "UNKNOWN" #>    focal_data: "" #>    bg_data: "" #>    focal_computed_objects: "" #>    bg_computed_objects: "" #>    content_key: "" #>    config: "" #>    extra_params: "" #>  footer-date-time: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z')`" #>  recipient-email: "`r params$recipient_email`" #>  --- #>   #>  ```{r setup, include=FALSE} #>  knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, #>                        fig.align = 'center', error = FALSE, results = 'asis') #>  ``` #>   #>  # Hello `r params$recipient_name`! #>   #>  You have recorded `r length(unique(params$focal_data$scientific_name))` species recently! #>   #>  ```{r table} #>  table_data <- params$focal_data[, c('date', 'species_group', 'scientific_name', 'common_name')] #>  names(table_data) <- c('Date', 'Group', 'Species', 'Common name') #>  knitr::kable(table_data) #>  ``` #>   #>  Best wishes,   #>  recorderFeedback

title: Generating Content
text: Content generation controlled config.yml. Key entries : computation_script_focal: Script computations focal (recipient-specific) data. computation_script_bg: Script background computations. recipient_select_script: Optional script selecting recipients render batch. content_template_file: RMarkdown template rendering feedback. html_template_file: HTML template styling output. default, computation script fields point "scripts/computation.R".

title: Generating Content
text: Computation scripts let prepare summaries metrics rendering. useful totals, rankings, trends, values want reuse templates. normal use, computations executed automatically part rendering. building custom scripts, core helper used internally: computation script define compute_objects(records_data) return object (often list) can used templates.

title: Generating Content
text: Start rendering single recipient check template logic running full batch. run whole batch:
code: # Use a known recipient_id from recipients_file rf_render_single(recipient_id = 1) batch_id <- "example_batch" rf_render_all(batch_id)

title: Generating Content
text: Batch rendering can filtered script referenced recipient_select_script. script define recipient_select(recipients, data, config) return one : NULL render everyone. logical vector one value per row recipients. vector recipient_id values render. data frame containing recipient_id, selected, optional skip_reason. example, render recipients least five records record others skipped: Skipped recipients remain renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv render_status = "skipped", HTML file produced .
code: recipient_select <- function(recipients, data, config) {   counts <- aggregate(list(n = data$recipient_id), by = list(recipient_id = data$recipient_id), FUN = length)   counts <- merge(recipients["recipient_id"], counts, by = "recipient_id", all.x = TRUE)   counts$n[is.na(counts$n)] <- 0    data.frame(     recipient_id = counts$recipient_id,     selected = counts$n >= 5,     skip_reason = ifelse(counts$n >= 5, NA_character_, "Fewer than 5 records"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }

what: Using Location-Based Feedback Instead of Individual-Based Feedback
title: Generating Content
text: can use recorderFeedback place-based reporting (example, “species recorded location X”) rather person-based reporting. main idea : Keep one row per email recipient recipients_file. Add location fields recipients (example location_id, location_name). Ensure records data includes matching location field. Change scripts/focal_filter.R focal data filtered location, recorder identity. Required recipient columns still recipient_id, name, email, can add extra columns needed filtering template content. Example focal_filter() location-based emails: place, rf_render_single() rf_render_all() work normal, email can now summarise records recipient’s location. source data coordinates instead location_id, can apply spatial filter focal_filter() using polygons bounding boxes recipient row.
code: focal_filter <- function(all_data, recipient_id) {   config <- config::get()   recipients <- read.csv(config$recipients_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    rec <- recipients[recipients$recipient_id == recipient_id, , drop = FALSE]   if (nrow(rec) == 0) {     stop("No recipient row for recipient_id = ", recipient_id)   }    # Each recipient row maps to a location of interest.   loc <- rec$location_id[1]    # Focal data now means all records in that location.   focal_data <- subset(all_data, location_id == loc)    focal_data }

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: recorderFeedback R package generating personalised, data-driven feedback. built wildlife recording citizen science projects, teams want give contributors useful feedback participants boost engagement. package helps turn repeatable workflow R, using power R packages data processing visualisation.

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: many recording schemes, data arrives continuously feedback contributors irregular. bottleneck usually time: preparing personalised feedback manually scale. recorderFeedback provides practical way close gap, contributors hear back consistently rather occasionally. intend replace peer--peer personal feedback, complement regular, data-driven updates scale.

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: rf_dispatch_smtp() now writes dispatch artifacts per batch: renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_log.csv: one row per send attempt. renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_summary.csv: run-level summary metrics. Recommended send sequence: Keep test_mode: TRUE config.yml. Run rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, dry_run = TRUE) validate rows files. Run rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id) test-recipient sends. Switch test_mode: FALSE ready, use rf_dispatch_smtp(batch_id, confirm_live_send = TRUE). run interrupted, rerun resume = TRUE skip recipients already marked Success dispatch_log.csv. Campaign controls: mail_subject_template supports recipient/campaign variables, e.g. "{{campaign_name}} update {{name}}". mail_body_prefix_template / mail_body_suffix_template inject optional HTML around rendered email using template variables. mail_attachments_col / mail_inline_images_col define optional meta_table.csv columns per-recipient file paths attachments inline media. preview_only preview_n preview_recipient_ids writes sampled previews renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_preview/.

Using targets::tar_meta(fields = "error") also tell targets failed corresponding error messages. Use rf_render_single(recipient_id) render individual feedback item view detailed error message shown. what: Dispatch feedback via SMTP email — rf_dispatch_smtp
title: Dispatch feedback via SMTP email — rf_dispatch_smtp
text: Sends rendered feedback files recipients via SMTP preflight checks, retries, rate limiting, dispatch logging.

title: Dispatch feedback via SMTP email — rf_dispatch_smtp
code: rf_dispatch_smtp(   batch_id,   dry_run = FALSE,   resume = TRUE,   confirm_live_send = FALSE,   max_retries = NULL,   retry_backoff_sec = NULL,   rate_per_minute = NULL,   preview_n = 0,   preview_recipient_ids = NULL,   preview_only = FALSE,   preview_dir = NULL )

title: Dispatch feedback via SMTP email — rf_dispatch_smtp
text: batch_id Character. Identifier batch. dry_run Logical. TRUE, send mail; validate write logs. resume Logical. TRUE, skip recipients already marked `Success` `renders/<batch_id>/dispatch_log.csv`. confirm_live_send Logical. Must TRUE send real recipients `test_mode` FALSE. max_retries Integer. Number retries first failed attempt. Defaults `config$mail_retry_max`. retry_backoff_sec Numeric. Base seconds exponential backoff. Defaults `config$mail_retry_backoff_sec`. rate_per_minute Numeric. Maximum send attempts per minute. Defaults `config$mail_rate_per_minute`. preview_n Integer. > 0, process first `preview_n` recipients. preview_recipient_ids Optional vector recipient IDs process. preview_only Logical. TRUE, write preview HTML files send. preview_dir Optional output directory preview HTML files.

title: Look in the R Markdown template and identify computed objects required in the code to render the template. — rf_look_for_computed_objects
text: list containing two elements: bg_computed_objects: character vector representing unique computed objects following "params$bg_computed_objects$". user_computed_objects: character vector representing unique computed objects following "params$user_computed_objects$".

text: x list data frame feedback results summarize. batch_id Character. Identifier feedback batch. recipient_data Data frame. Recipient information join. selection_data Data frame. Recipient selection information.

text: Character. Path written meta table CSV file.

what: Preflight validation before render or dispatch — rf_preflight
title: Preflight validation before render or dispatch — rf_preflight
text: Runs early checks fail fast expensive render email steps.

title: Preflight validation before render or dispatch — rf_preflight
code: rf_preflight(   stage = c("render", "dispatch"),   batch_id = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   check_recorder_schema = TRUE,   recorder_schema_cols = c("date", "species", "site"),   recorder_schema_required = FALSE )

title: Preflight validation before render or dispatch — rf_preflight
text: stage Character. One `"render"` `"dispatch"`. batch_id Character. Required `stage = "dispatch"`. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages. check_recorder_schema Logical. Passed [rf_verify_data()]. recorder_schema_cols Character vector recorder schema columns. recorder_schema_required Logical. Passed [rf_verify_data()].

title: Preflight validation before render or dispatch — rf_preflight
text: Invisible TRUE checks pass, otherwise stops error.

text: batch_id Character. Identifier batch verify. verbose Logical, TRUE prints summary info.

text: Invisible TRUE checks pass, otherwise stops warns.

what: Verify data and recipient files — rf_verify_data
title: Verify data and recipient files — rf_verify_data
text: Checks raw data file recipient file exist, readable, contain required columns, validates recipient IDs emails, can optionally check recorder-analytics schema columns.

title: Verify data and recipient files — rf_verify_data
code: rf_verify_data(   verbose = FALSE,   check_recorder_schema = FALSE,   recorder_schema_cols = c("date", "species", "site"),   recorder_schema_required = FALSE )

title: Verify data and recipient files — rf_verify_data
text: verbose Logical, TRUE prints number records recipients. check_recorder_schema Logical. TRUE, validate recorder schema columns. recorder_schema_cols Character vector optional recorder schema columns. Defaults c("date", "species", "site"). recorder_schema_required Logical. TRUE, missing recorder schema columns treated errors. FALSE, missing columns trigger warnings.

title: Verify data and recipient files — rf_verify_data
text: Invisible TRUE checks pass, otherwise stops error.
